2		import * as d3 from 'd3'
3		import { ZoomIn, ZoomOut, Maximize2 } from 'lucide-svelte'
4	
5		interface GraphNode {
6			id: string
7			path: string
8			title: string
9			dependsOn: string[]
10			dependents: string[]
11			staleness?: string
12			mediaType?: string
13		}
14	
15		interface GraphLink {
16			source: string
17			target: string
18		}
19	
20		interface ZoomTransform {
21			x: number
22			y: number
23			k: number
24		}
25	
26		let {
27			nodes,
28			links,
29			onNodeClick,
30			initialTransform = null,
31			onTransformChange,
32			highlightNodeId = undefined
33		}: {
34			nodes: GraphNode[]
35			links: GraphLink[]
36			onNodeClick?: (node: GraphNode) => void
37			initialTransform?: ZoomTransform | null
38			onTransformChange?: (t: ZoomTransform) => void
39			highlightNodeId?: string
40		} = $props()
41	
42		let svgEl = $state<SVGSVGElement | undefined>(undefined)
43		let tooltipNode = $state<GraphNode | null>(null)
44		let tooltipX = $state(0)
45		let tooltipY = $state(0)
46	
47		// Zoom behavior reference — set inside $effect, used by button handlers
48		let zoom: d3.ZoomBehavior<SVGSVGElement, unknown> | null = null
49	
50		function zoomIn() {
51			if (!svgEl || !zoom) return
52			d3.select(svgEl).transition().duration(250).call(zoom.scaleBy, 1.4)
53		}
54	
55		function zoomOut() {
56			if (!svgEl || !zoom) return
57			d3.select(svgEl).transition().duration(250).call(zoom.scaleBy, 1 / 1.4)
58		}
59	
60		function resetZoom() {
61			if (!svgEl || !zoom) return
62			d3.select(svgEl).transition().duration(350).call(zoom.transform, d3.zoomIdentity)
63		}
64	
65		function nodeColorClass(staleness?: string): string {
66			if (staleness === 'stale') return 'node-stale'
67			if (staleness === 'missing') return 'node-missing'
68			// fresh (default)
69			return 'node-fresh'
70		}
71	
72		$effect(() => {
73			const el: SVGSVGElement | undefined = svgEl
74			if (!el) return
75	
76			const currentNodes = nodes
77			const currentLinks = links
78			const capturedInitialTransform = initialTransform
79			const capturedHighlightId = highlightNodeId
80	
81			type SimNode = GraphNode & d3.SimulationNodeDatum
82	
83			let simulation: d3.Simulation<SimNode, { source: string; target: string }> | null = null
84			let currentWidth = 0
85			let currentHeight = 0
86	
87			function rebuild(svgNode: SVGSVGElement, width: number, height: number) {
88				currentWidth = width
89				currentHeight = height
90				simulation?.stop()
91	
92				const svgSel = d3.select(svgNode)
93				svgSel.selectAll('*').remove()
94	
95				svgSel
96					.append('defs')
97					.append('marker')
98					.attr('id', 'ddx-arrow')
99					.attr('viewBox', '0 -5 10 10')
100					.attr('refX', 28)
101					.attr('refY', 0)
102					.attr('markerWidth', 6)
103					.attr('markerHeight', 6)
104					.attr('orient', 'auto')
105					.append('path')
106					.attr('d', 'M0,-5L10,0L0,5')
107					.attr('class', 'fill-fg-muted dark:fill-dark-fg-muted')
108	
109				const g = svgSel.append('g')
110	
111				zoom = d3
112					.zoom<SVGSVGElement, unknown>()
113					.scaleExtent([0.05, 10])
114					.on('zoom', (event) => {
115						g.attr('transform', event.transform.toString())
116					})
117					.on('end', (event) => {
118						if (onTransformChange) {
119							onTransformChange({
120								x: event.transform.x,
121								y: event.transform.y,
122								k: event.transform.k
123							})
124						}
125					})
126	
127				svgSel.call(zoom)
128				svgSel.on('dblclick.zoom', null)
129	
130				// Apply initialTransform immediately if provided
131				if (capturedInitialTransform) {
132					svgSel.call(
133						zoom.transform,
134						d3.zoomIdentity
135							.translate(capturedInitialTransform.x, capturedInitialTransform.y)
136							.scale(capturedInitialTransform.k)
137					)
138				}
139	
140				const freshSimNodes: SimNode[] = currentNodes.map((n) => ({ ...n }))
141				const freshNodeById = new Map(freshSimNodes.map((n) => [n.id, n]))
142				const freshSimLinks = currentLinks
143					.filter((l) => freshNodeById.has(l.source) && freshNodeById.has(l.target))
144					.map((l) => ({ source: l.source, target: l.target }))
145	
146				simulation = d3
147					.forceSimulation(freshSimNodes)
148					.force(
149						'link',
150						d3
151							.forceLink<SimNode, (typeof freshSimLinks)[0]>(freshSimLinks)
152							.id((d) => d.id)
153							.distance(160)
154							.strength(0.4)
155					)
156					.force('charge', d3.forceManyBody().strength(-600))
157					.force('center', d3.forceCenter(width / 2, height / 2))
158					.force('collide', d3.forceCollide(48))
159	
160				const linkSel = g
161					.append('g')
162					.selectAll<SVGLineElement, (typeof freshSimLinks)[0]>('line')
163					.data(freshSimLinks)
164					.join('line')
165					.attr('class', 'stroke-fg-muted dark:stroke-dark-fg-muted')
166					.attr('stroke-width', 1.5)
167					.attr('stroke-opacity', 0.6)
168					.attr('marker-end', 'url(#ddx-arrow)')
169	
170				const nodeGroup = g
171					.append('g')
172					.selectAll<SVGGElement, SimNode>('g')
173					.data(freshSimNodes)
174					.join('g')
175					.style('cursor', onNodeClick ? 'pointer' : 'grab')
176	
177				nodeGroup
178					.append('circle')
179					.attr('r', 18)
180					.attr('class', (d: SimNode) => nodeColorClass(d.staleness))
181					.attr('stroke-width', (d: SimNode) => (d.id === capturedHighlightId ? 4 : 2))
182	
183				nodeGroup
184					.append('text')
185					.attr('x', 24)
186					.attr('dy', '0.35em')
187					.attr('class', 'fill-fg-muted dark:fill-dark-fg-muted select-none text-body-sm')
188					.attr('pointer-events', 'none')
189					.text((d) => (d.title.length > 32 ? d.title.slice(0, 32) + '…' : d.title))
190	
191				// Drag with distance tracking to distinguish click from drag
192				let dragDistance = 0
193	
194				const drag = d3
195					.drag<SVGGElement, SimNode>()
196					.on('start', (event, d) => {
197						dragDistance = 0
198						if (!event.active) simulation!.alphaTarget(0.3).restart()
199						d.fx = d.x
200						d.fy = d.y

input.command:
git add cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(web): e2e regression for doc graph edge color contrast [ddx-6019fa66]

Adds TC-042 to graph.spec.ts: toggles light/dark and asserts the doc graph
edge stroke (with stroke-opacity blended) and arrowhead fill meet WCAG 3:1
non-text contrast against the canvas tokens.

The test surfaced two gaps in the upstream contrast fix:

- Tailwind v4 does not generate stroke-* / fill-* utilities for the custom
  fg-muted tokens defined via theme.extend.colors, so the previous class
  swap painted as default black, not the chosen ink. Switch the line and
  arrowhead to text-fg-muted dark:text-dark-fg-muted with stroke/fill set
  to currentColor — that utility is generated and inherits correctly.
- Edge stroke at opacity 0.6 over bg-canvas blended to ~2.7:1, below the
  3:1 floor. Bumping to 0.85 brings light mode above 3:1 while keeping
  the visual de-emphasis vs. node strokes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -20

data.text:
Committed. TC-042 passes locally (light + dark contrast both ≥3:1). Pre-existing TC-040 flake confirmed unrelated.

Summary:
- Added `TC-042` to `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/graph.spec.ts`: reads computed stroke/fill from a real edge + arrowhead in both themes, blends stroke against the canvas token, and asserts WCAG ≥3:1.
- Test exposed that Tailwind v4 doesn't emit `stroke-*`/`fill-*` for the custom `fg-muted` tokens, so the upstream contrast fix was painting black; switched edge/arrow to `text-fg-muted dark:text-dark-fg-muted` + `currentColor`.
- Bumped `stroke-opacity` 0.6 → 0.85 so the blended light-mode stroke clears 3:1.
- Updated the existing unit contrast spec to assert the new approach.
